返回本批次

Moleskine的AI指环王合集只能嘲笑

Moleskine's AI Lord of the Rings collection can only mock

cjleo.com 作者 lentil_soup 分数 73 评论 100 2026/04/27 17:19
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 笔记本电脑公司 Moleskine 今天宣布推出新的《指环王》系列。他们的网站和社交媒体上的帖子展示了一套笔记本和记事本，以及带有彩色指环王艺术品的商品（别针、补丁、贴纸和明信片）。问题？一些图像上有一个小免责声明，上面写着：“由 Moleskine 想象，由人工智能生成。”自从该品牌声称他们的传奇笔记本曾经被欧内斯特·海明威和巴勃罗·毕加索等名人​​使用过以来，我已经使用 Moleskine 笔记本很多年了。如今，他们已经调整了声明，只承认这一说法，但不做出承诺，这似乎是他们在当前人工智能争议中继续使用的营销策略。“Moleskine 笔记本是过去两个世纪艺术家和思想家使用的传奇笔记本的继承者和继承者：其中包括文森特·梵高、巴勃罗·毕加索、欧内斯特·海明威和布鲁斯·查特文。” ——Moleskine网站上的“我们的遗产：传奇笔记本” 不过，“传奇”这个词在他们的网站上大量出现：从“传奇交易”到“传奇收藏”。我也是《指环王》的忠实粉丝，所以这个系列立刻引起了我的注意。今天早上，我在 Instagram 上保存了这篇帖子，以便回家后一定要看看。但当我终于有机会在 Moleskine 网站上浏览该系列产品时，有些事情阻止了我下订单。一开始我很难确定它是什么，但后来，在网站上的宣传图片一角，我看到了人工智能免责声明。此时，Moleskine 在宣布《指环王》系列的 Instagram 帖子中出现了几条评论，呼吁 Moleskine 使用人工智能，但 Moleskine 尚未对此做出回应。事实上，Moleskine 在 2026 年 4 月 15 日发布的 Instagram 公告中的八张照片中没有任何提及人工智能。广告没有详细说明产品的哪些部分是人工智能，或者如何使用人工智能。在人工智能的早期阶段，人们甚至没有任何可以发现的线索。这是一次合法的合作，产品上印有《指环王》的标志和商标。Moleskine 的《指环王》系列中的艺术作品具有扁平、简约的风格，没有嘈杂的像素或解剖异常。圣盔谷和刚铎的设计很通用，缺乏细节。封面图像中的所有内容都是用块色和轮廓完成的。一方面，这是隐藏一件艺术品是人工智能生成的可靠策略。另一方面，有些艺术家确实在他们合法的人造艺术品中使用了这种风格。这就是问题所在：我们无法区分其中的区别。如果没有“AI 生成”的注释，可能无法判断 Moleskine 笔记本收藏中的艺术品是否是 AI。截至撰写本文时，Moleskine 尚未承认在这些封面设计中使用了人工智能，但他们也没有注明艺术家或提供任何人类创作的证据。鉴于之前的限量版系列往往都是与艺术家和设计师合作，而 Moleskine 在其宣传材料中自豪地将其放在前面和中心位置，这令人不安。Moleskine 2024 年的“3 位艺术家，3 个国家”系列以曾梵志、安尚秀和佐仓桃子的设计为特色。更令人不安的是，因为 Moleskine 品牌吸引创意人士，似乎很重视支持“受《指环王》的启发，这个系列提醒我们，即使我们在广阔的世界中感到渺小，我们每个人都拥有帮助我们坚持下去的创造力。​通过用笔写在纸上，你可以在探索自己独特的想象力时找到清晰的方向。前进的道路就在你的笔尖。” — Moleskine 网站上每款《指环王》产品下方的简介广告事实上，在售的三款笔记本的产品描述非常相似（下面每个描述中的单个更改短语的重点是我的）。所有地点都是“若隐若现的背景”，每件产品都以具有安静或谦逊优势的角色为特色。“莱戈拉斯和吉姆利在这本 18 个月计划的封面上可能显得渺小而孤独，但他们在圣盔谷若隐若现的背景下脱颖而出——提醒人们，即使是最安静的力量也能塑造世界。” —《指环王》规划师 2026/2027，Moleskine 网站“阿拉贡在这本笔记本的封面上可能显得渺小而孤独，但他在刚铎城若隐若现的背景下脱颖而出——提醒人们，即使是最安静的个人力量也能塑造世界。” ——《指环王笔记本》，Moleskine 网站“在这本笔记本的封面上，弗罗多和山姆可能显得渺小而孤独，但他们在魔多迫在眉睫的背景下脱颖而出——提醒人们，即使是最卑微的力量也能塑造世界。” — 《指环王笔记本》，Moleskine 网站广告 目前，还没有关于在销售产品中使用人工智能的法规。没有要求公司声明在其产品中使用人工智能艺术。 “AI”或“人工智能”一词甚至没有出现在 Moleskine 品牌网站上的道德准则中。 Moleskine 的人工智能免责声明仅出现在三个横幅图像上，而不是出现在任何单独的产品页面上，而在这些页面上，通知消费者可能是最重要的。《指环王》粉丝应该非常确定的一件事是，宣传材料中使用了人工智能。 Moleskine 在《指环王》系列发布会上发布到 Instagram 上的八张图片中的最后一张是一张标志性托尔金风格的地图。但该地图显示了诸如“Der Rarmorth”和“Narmimtz”等无意义的地点。Moleskine 通过 Instagram 发布的图片宣传该系列，其特点是一张《指环王》风格的地图，带有无意义的文本，并且没有人工智能免责声明。Moleskine 网站横幅背景中的另一张地图显示了中土世界中不存在的其他乱码地点的名称。这也表明 Moleskine 对人工智能的标签不一致，如果是善意的话：第一张地图没有标注人工智能免责声明，但第二张地图却有。还有这个笔记本，上面有一张地图，它包含在 Instagram 帖子中，但在网站上找不到。这张地图太模糊了，看不清，但地理看起来与中土世界的其他地图不一致。这些地图是怎么溜过去的？人们会希望团队里有一个对《指环王》有一点了解的人来设计和批准这次活动的广告。这就是 Moleskine 的嘲讽，不仅是对《指环王》粉丝的嘲笑，也是对他们自己的客户和世界各地富有创造力的人们的嘲笑，他们声称支持这些人。现在有多少品牌正在制作人工智能艺术作品，通过模仿美学来利用粉丝对其粉丝群体的热情，而不需要在项目中投入任何努力或热情？我们能确定吗？不到一年前，即 2025 年 5 月，Moleskine 时任首席执行官 Christophe Archaimbault 告诉《福布斯》，“我们的使命是在纸上释放人类天才。” 2026 年 2 月阿尚博下台后，新领导层似乎并没有将其声明中的“人”部分作为优先事项。Moleskine 的宣言，可在他们的网站上找到。不过，Moleskine 在他们的宣言中赞扬了“手写的永恒力量”，最后以简洁的鼓励“把笔写在纸上，释放你独特的声音”。生成式人工智能不可能是独一无二的，因为它经常在未经许可的情况下使用人类艺术家的艺术作品。“孕育[兽人]的影子只能嘲笑，它不能创造：它自己并不是真正的新事物。” — J.R.R.托尔金《王者归来》截至撰写本文时，Moleskine 尚未就他们在该系列中使用人工智能的情况发表声明。与此同时，有人推荐一款好的笔记本吗？广告更新2026 年 4 月 17 日Moleskine 在其 Instagram 帖子上发表评论，宣布于 2026 年 4 月 17 日推出《指环王》系列。感谢您的评论。我们只是想确认所有 Moleskine 笔记本封面都是由我们的设计师创作的，而人工智能则用于增强这些图像的背景。我们希望《指环王》能够给您带来启发！这份声明仅出现在 Moleskine 的 Instagram 上，而非其官方网站上，声称笔记本封面是由设计师创作的，并且人工智能用于宣传图片的背景。但这并不是对使用人工智能的道歉，Moleskine 也没有从其社交媒体或网站上删除人工智能生成的图像。封面设计是设计师在没有使用人工智能的情况下创建的，这也不是完全透明的说法。 Moleskine 没有提供封面完全是人造艺术品的证据，也没有透露封面设计师的名字。Moleskine 甚至在宣传图片中也使用了人工智能，而且他们对人类艺术家和使用其产品的创意人员的担忧缺乏责任感和纠正措施，这表明他们对自己的品牌存在令人震惊的误解，也不尊重与其合作的品牌，例如《指环王》及其粉丝群体。 2026Moleskine 删除了其网站上所有图像上的“Moleskine 想象，AI 生成”免责声明。人工智能生成的宣传图片继续存在于其网站上，但没有免责声明。考虑到他们已经承认在 Instagram 上的这些图像中使用了人工智能，在他们的网站上变得不那么透明是一个令人费解的举动。这种加倍的努力是对创意人员、他们的主要客户群和《指环王》粉丝的侮辱，明显是试图掩盖他们在这些宣传图像中使用人工智能的事实，特别是对那些没有在社交媒体上看到他们声明的人。《指环王 Moleskine》系列仍然向会员出售，三本书合集售价 175 美元已经卖完了。Moleskine 尚未证实笔记本电脑封面（其中一些包含不可思议的地图）是由人类设计师创建的，在整个过程的任何部分都没有使用人工智能。

Original Summary

  1. Moleskine, the notebook company, today announced their new The Lord of the Rings collection. Posts on their website and social media show a set of notebooks and planners, along with merchandise (pins, patches, stickers and postcards) with colourful Lord of the Rings artwork. The problem? A small disclaimer on some of the images reads, “Imagined by Moleskine, generated by AI.”I have used Moleskine notebooks for years, since the days when the brand claimed that their legendary notebooks were once used by luminaries such as Ernest Hemingway and Pablo Picasso. Nowadays, they have tempered their statement to only acknowledge the claim without committing to it, a marketing strategy that they appear to continue using in the current AI controversy.“The Moleskine notebook is the heir and successor to the legendary notebook used by artists and thinkers over the past two centuries: among them Vincent Van Gogh, Pablo Picasso, Ernest Hemingway and Bruce Chatwin.” —”Our Heritage: The Legendary Notebook” on Moleskine’s websiteStill, the word “legendary” appears on their website in abundance: from “legendary deals” to “legendary collection.”I am also a big Lord of the Rings fan, so this collection immediately caught my eye. This morning, I saved the post on Instagram to make sure to take a look when I got home. But when I finally had the chance to browse the collection on the Moleskine website, something held me back from placing an order. It was hard to put my finger on it at first, but then, in the corner of a promotional image on the website, I saw the AI disclaimer.By this time, Moleskine’s Instagram post announcing the Lord of the Rings collection had several comments calling out Moleskine for the AI use, to which Moleskine have not responded. In fact, none of the eight pictures in Moleskine’s Instagram announcement post, on 15 April 2026, had any mention of AI whatsoever.advertisementThere are no details about which parts of the product are AI, or how AI was used. There aren’t even any clues that one could have picked out in the earlier days of AI. And this is a legitimate collaboration, with The Lord of the Rings logo and trademark stamped on the products.The art in Moleskine’s The Lord of the Rings collection has a flat, minimalist style that doesn’t have noisy pixels or anatomical anomalies. The designs of Helm’s Deep and Gondor are generic and lack details. Everything in the cover images is done in block colours and silhouettes. On one hand, this is a solid strategy to hide that a piece of artwork was AI-generated. On the other, there are artists out there who do use this sort of style in their legitimate, human-made artwork.And that’s the problem: we cannot tell the difference. Without the “generated by AI” note, there might be no way to tell if the art in Moleskine’s notebook collection is AI. As of writing, Moleskine have not admitted to using AI in these cover designs, but they also have not credited an artist or provided any proof of human creation. Given that previous limited-edition collections have often been collaborations with artists and designers that Moleskine have proudly featured front and centre in their promotional material, this is troubling.Moleskine’s “3 Artists, 3 Countries” collection in 2024 featured designs by Zeng Fanzhi, Ahn Sang Soo and Momoko Sakura.It’s even more disturbing because the Moleskine brand appeals to creatives and seems to place importance on championing creativity.“Inspired by The Lord of the Rings, this collection reminds us that even when we feel small in a vast world, each of us has creative talents that help us persevere.​ By putting pen to paper, you can find clarity as you explore your own unique imagination. The way forward is at the tip of your pen.” — The blurb beneath each of The Lord of the Rings products on Moleskine’s websiteadvertisementIn fact, the product descriptions for the three notebooks on sale are suspiciously similar (emphases on the single changed phrase in each description below are mine). All the locations are a “looming background”, and each product features characters that have quiet or humble strengths.“Legolas and Gimli may appear small and alone on the cover of this 18-month planner, yet they stand out against the looming background of Helm’s Deep – a reminder of how even the quietest of strengths can shape the world.” — The Lord of the Rings Planner 2026/2027, Moleskine’s website“Aragorn may appear small and alone on the cover of this notebook, yet he stands out against the looming background of the city of Gondor – a reminder of how even the quietest individual strengths can shape the world.” — The Lord of the Rings Notebook, Moleskine’s website“Frodo and Sam may appear small and alone on the cover of this notebook, yet they stand out against the looming background of Mordor – a reminder of how even the humblest of strengths can shape the world.” — The Lord of the Rings Notebook, Moleskine’s websiteadvertisementCurrently, there are no regulations on AI use in products for sale. There are no requirements that a company declare AI art use in their products. The words “AI” or “artificial intelligence” don’t even appear in Moleskine’s Code of Ethics on the brand’s website. Moleskine’s AI disclaimer is only on three banner images and isn’t on any of the individual product pages, where it might be most important to inform the consumer.One thing Lord of the Rings fans should be pretty certain about, AI was used in the promotional material. The last of eight images posted to Instagram by Moleskine in the announcement of The Lord of the Rings collection features a map in the iconic Tolkien style. But the map shows nonsensical locations like “Der Rarmorth” and “Narmimtz”.Image from Moleskine via Instagram promoting the collection features a map in the style of The Lord of the Rings with nonsensical text and no AI disclaimer.Another map in the background of a banner on the Moleskine website shows other gibberish names of places that don’t exist in Middle-earth. This also shows an inconsistency in Moleskine’s labelling of AI, if in good faith: the first map was not marked with the AI disclaimer, but the second was.There is also this notebook that features a map, which is included in the Instagram post but is nowhere to be found on the website. The map is too blurry to make out properly, but the geography looks inconsistent with other maps of Middle-earth.How did these maps slip through? One would hope that someone who even tangentially knew The Lord of the Rings would be on the team designing and approving the ads for this campaign.advertisementTherein lies Moleskine’s mockery, not only of fans of The Lord of the Rings, but also of their own customers and creative humans everywhere, whom they claim to support. How many brands are now generating AI artwork to capitalise on the fervour fans have for their fandoms by mimicking an aesthetic without pouring any effort or passion into the project? Will we be able to know for certain?Less than a year ago, in May 2025, then-CEO of Moleskine Christophe Archaimbault told Forbes, “Our mission is to unleash human genius on paper.” After Achaimbault stepped down in February 2026, new leadership appears not to be making the “human” part of his statement a priority.Moleskine’s Manifesto, found on their website.Still, in their Manifesto, Moleskine extol the “timeless power of handwriting” before ending with a pithy encouragement to “put pen to paper, and unleash your unique voice.” Generative AI cannot be unique, as it uses, often without permission, the artwork of human artists.“The Shadow that bred [the orcs] can only mock, it cannot make: not real new things of its own.” — J.R.R. Tolkien, The Return of the KingAs of writing, Moleskine have not made a statement about their AI use in this collection.In the meantime, does anyone have recommendations for a good notebook?advertisementUpdate17 April 2026Moleskine posted a comment on their Instagram post announcing The Lord of the Rings collection on 17 April 2026.Thank you for your comments. We just wanted to confirm that all Moleskine notebook covers are created by our designers, while AI was used to enhance the background of these images. We hope The Lord of the Rings inspires you!This statement, which is only on Moleskine’s Instagram and not on their official website, claims that the notebook covers were created by designers, and AI was used for the backgrounds of the promotional images.However, it is not an apology for using AI, and Moleskine have not removed the AI-generated images from their social media or their website. It is also not a fully transparent statement that the cover designs were created by the designers without the use of AI. Moleskine have not provided evidence of completely human-made artwork for the covers or named the designers who worked on them.Moleskine’s use of AI even in promotional images, and their lack of accountability and corrective action in response to the concerns of human artists and the creatives who use their product, shows a shocking misunderstanding of their own brand and a disrespect for the brands it collaborates with, such as The Lord of the Rings and its fandom.advertisement25 April 2026Moleskine have removed the “Imagined by Moleskine, generated by AI” disclaimer on all images on their website. The AI-generated promotional images continue to exist on their website, without the disclaimer. Considering they have already admitted to using AI in these images on Instagram, it’s a baffling move to become less transparent on their website.This doubling down is insulting to creatives, their main customer base, and fans of The Lord of the Rings, in a clear attempt to hide the fact that they have used AI in those promotional images, particularly from those who have not seen their statement on social media.The Lord of the Rings Moleskine collection remains on sale to members, with the $175 3-book collection already sold out. Moleskine have not confirmed that the notebook covers, some of which contain the uncanny maps, were created by human designers without the use of AI in any part of the process.

评论（默认中文）

wccrawford 2026/04/27 19:53
“还有一本笔记本，上面有一张地图，它包含在 Instagram 帖子中，但在网站上找不到。地图太模糊，无法正确辨认，但地理看起来与中土世界的其他地图不一致。”

绝对不合情理。

广告必须真实。 他们不能凭空捏造产品中实际上没有的东西。 这实际上是虚假广告。

我不反对人工智能，但我反对欺骗人。 如果你懒得去实际检查人工智能的输出，那么你就不应该使用它。

"There is also this notebook that features a map, which is included in the Instagram post but is nowhere to be found on the website. The map is too blurry to make out properly, but the geography looks inconsistent with other maps of Middle-earth."

Absolutely unconscionable.

Ads need to be truthful. They can't just make things up that aren't actually in the product. It's literally false advertising.

I'm not against AI, but I am against deceiving people. If you can't be bothered to actually check your AI's output, you shouldn't be using it.

Clicking the image expands it. Looks like the real thing to me (and easy enough when you've got the rights; to use AI for this would have been idiotic)

> “还有这个笔记本，上面有一张地图，它包含在 Instagram 帖子中，但在网站上找不到。地图太模糊，无法正确辨认，但地理看起来与中土世界的其他地图不一致。”

现在是 2026 年。我让人工智能给了我两段指环王的摘要，这样我就可以了解它。然后我让它检查地图，它说没问题，所以我很高兴。

> "There is also this notebook that features a map, which is included in the Instagram post but is nowhere to be found on the website. The map is too blurry to make out properly, but the geography looks inconsistent with other maps of Middle-earth."

It's very 2026. I had my AI give me a two paragraph summary of the LOTR, so I could be knowledgeable about it. Then I had it check the map, and it said it was fine, so I'm happy.

Since when? Ads are full of made up shit. Even the products are often presented in a way where it's obvious that something is not real.

事实证明，OP 的帖子有来自他们的更新：

> 感谢您的评论。我们只是想确认所有 Moleskine 笔记本封面都是由我们的设计师创作的，而人工智能则用于增强这些图像的背景。我们希望《指环王》能够给您带来启发！

Turns out it is exactly that, the OP's post has an update from them:

> Thank you for your comments. We just wanted to confirm that all Moleskine notebook covers are created by our designers, while AI was used to enhance the background of these images. We hope The Lord of the Rings inspires you!

securicat 2026/04/27 19:51
是的，我只是真的不明白其中的问题（并不是说你明白或不明白）。如果是我，我是他们，我会和艺术家签约，因为只有几张图片，这样可以避免争议。不过，我也使用图像生成工具来制作有趣的非商业作品，如果没有这些工具，我永远不会完成这些作品。

这会让我更有创造力还是更少？我不知道。

Yeah I just really don’t see the issue in that (not saying you do or don’t either). If it were me and I were them I would have contracted with artists because it’s only a few images and it would avoid controversy. However I also use image generation tools for fun non-commercial pieces that I never would have done without the tools.

Does that make me more creative or less? I’m not sure.

sheiyei 2026/04/27 20:28

HWR_14 2026/04/27 21:15

但当人工智能重新创作你正在考虑购买的艺术品时，情况似乎更加极端。

I don't see how replacing a photographer with AI for advertising is considerably different from replacing a photographer of a nature scene with AI. Is it just "AI is okay for commercial uses"?

But it seems more extreme when the AI is recreating the art you are considering buying.

Kye 2026/04/27 23:17

Kye 2026/04/27 21:56

在 HN 中查看
nkrisc 2026/04/27 20:40
因为现有的文化对艺术的理解是有人花时间创造了你正在经历的东西。人工智能生成的“艺术”颠覆了这种期望。感觉很欺骗。说实话，这让我想起了杜尚的作品 喷泉 和类似的作品，有些人 讨厌的 出于或多或少相同的原因。

然而，我并没有将人工智能的废液与马塞尔·杜尚等同起来。他的作品和他所做的一切都是有意引起的反应。

Because the existing cultural understanding of art is that someone took the time to create what you’re experiencing. AI generated “art” subverts that expectation. It feels deceptive. Honestly it reminds of Duchamp’s Fountain and similar works, which some people hated for more or less the same reason.

I am not equating AI slop with Marcel Duchamp, however. His work and what he did was very much intentional to evoke the sort of reactions it did.

readthenotes1 2026/04/28 00:08

The AI art debate reminds me a bit of the blowback from Miles Davis, a famous at the time jazz musician, recording Sketches of Spain, which is not jazz.

His response was "it's music, and I like it".

Some of those pictures? I like them.

semiquaver 2026/04/27 20:39
我觉得这篇文章令人困惑。 通常人们会愤怒地要求查明有人在制作产品模型时是否使用了 Photoshop 吗？如果不是，为什么会有所不同？只有当你做出政治决定，认为任何与人工智能相关的东西都是不好的，这才有意义，无论你是否喜欢它。
I find this post baffling. Would one normally demand angrily to find out whether someone used photoshop when making a product mockup? If not, why is this different? It only makes sense if you’ve made a political decision that anything associated with AI is bad, regardless of whether you would otherwise like it.
在 HN 中查看
f6v 2026/04/27 20:43
艺术家们对任何人工智能都抱有强烈的蔑视。我已经多次看到此类评论，就像人们对有人在 Discord 中使用人工智能生成的个人资料图片感到不安一样。

我知道他们很害怕。他们应该是：即使在人工智能出现之前，许多艺术家也很难谋生。市场已经过度饱和，他们不得不接受低薪的非正规工作。但现在对于 99.9% 的艺术家来说，隧道尽头几乎没有光明。

话虽如此，抵制人工智能的使用不会让他们一事无成。

There're a strong disdain agains any AI among the artists. I've seen these kind of comments many times, like people getting upset someone uses AI-generated profile pic in Discord.

I get that they're scared. They should be: it was difficult to make a living for many artists even before AI. The market was already oversaturated and they had to accept low-paying irregular jobs. But now there's literally no light in the end of the tunnel for 99.9% of artists.

That being said, boycotting AI use will get them nowhere.

在 HN 中查看
ottah 2026/04/27 22:27
我想说的是，那些以创作艺术为生的人有强烈的蔑视，但对于该群体之外的艺术家来说，这种蔑视并不普遍。我确实讨厌那些对某项活动有金钱利益的人被认为是唯一具有有效利益的人。自扩散模型以来，我看到越来越多的人对个人创意表达感兴趣，这是我一生中从未见过的，这是一件好事。
I'd say there's strong disdain from people who make a living producing art, but it's not nearly as universal with artists outside that group. I do resent people who have a monetary interest in an activity being presented as the only ones with a valid interest. I have seen more people becoming interested in personal creative expression since diffusion models, than I have ever seen before in my life, and that's a good thing.
在 HN 中查看
rsynnott 2026/04/27 21:28
嗯，我的意思是，输出通常很糟糕。

（人工智能“艺术”还有一个令人着迷的，而且可能是独特的特性，你看它越多，它就越糟糕；它非常糟糕，因为它的坏处比其他任何地方都更容易在小细节中突然出现。因此，我实际上有点喜欢小剂量的它；玩“发现破损的东西”很有趣。）

Well, also, I mean, the output is generally bad.

(AI 'art' also has the fascinating, and possibly unique, property that, the more you look at it, the worse it is; it is fractally bad, in that the badness tends to crop up in small details more than anywhere else. I actually kind of enjoy it in small doses for this reason; it's fun to play "spot the broken thing".)

在 HN 中查看
madamelic 2026/04/27 21:08
我在其他地方读到的内容是“如果有人使用人工智能化身，他们永远不会成为你的客户”。

我过去每年或两年都会委托一位特定的艺术家创作化身。它并不是非常便宜（数百美元）。但归根结底，花费数百美元，等待数周，然后可能得到我想要的 85% 的东西是没有意义的，因为我可以花 0 美元，等待 30 秒，得到我想要的 98% 的东西。

在我看来，艺术家应该在“堆栈”中向上移动。如果他们是委托艺术家，他们应该让客户以“98％的努力”来找他们，或者只接受对人工智能来说意味着太多、太复杂或其他敏感的委托。

人类想要艺术。人类都喜欢漂亮的东西。人工智能永远无法取代艺术家的全部需求。我认为这是摆脱了糟糕的专员（对价格敏感的、乞丐等），让人们更容易表达自己，从而使艺术家的工作更容易从他们的专员那里获取信息。

Something I read elsewhere was "if someone is using an AI avatar, they were never going to be your customer anyway".

I used to commission avatars every year or two from a specific artist. It wasn't super cheap (hundreds of dollars). At the end of the day though, spending hundreds of dollars, waiting weeks, and then maybe getting 85% of what I wanted doesn't make sense when I could instead spend ~$0, wait 30 seconds, and get 98% of what I want.

In my view, artists should be moving up the 'stack'. If they are a commission artist, they should be having customers come to them with their '98% efforts' or only taking on commissions that either mean too much, too elaborate for AI, or otherwise sensitive.

Humans want art. Humans love pretty things. AI will never replace the entire need for artists. I see it as getting rid of the bad commissioners (price sensitive, beggars, etc) and making it easier for people to express themselves thereby making an artist's job easier to extract info from their commissioners.

在 HN 中查看
Ruthalas 2026/04/27 21:36
这对我的思维方式来说非常陌生。

我定期委托艺术家，如果我必须说出最重要的两个原因，他们会是 1）我真的很喜欢他们的风格，并且想要这种风格的作品 2）我想支持他们，以便他们可以继续创作我喜欢的艺术。

满足我的包含清单很重要，但绝对是上述原因的次要因素。 （有时这些偏差反映了艺术家的特定风格，因此受到欢迎。）

This is very foreign to my way of thinking.

I commission artists somewhat regularly, and if I had to name the top two reasons, they would be 1) I really like their style, and want a piece in that style 2) I want to support them so they can continue making the art I like.

Meeting my checklist of inclusions is important, but definitely secondary to the reasons above. (And sometimes the deviations are reflections of the artist's particular style and therefore welcome.)

在 HN 中查看
palmotea 2026/04/28 01:43
> 我在其他地方读到的内容是“如果有人使用人工智能化身，他们永远不会成为你的客户”。

你的观点是你引用的内容是假的吗？

> 我过去每年或两年都会委托一位特定艺术家创作化身。它并不是非常便宜（数百美元）。但归根结底，花费数百美元，等待数周，然后可能得到我想要的 85% 的东西是没有意义的，因为我可以花 0 美元，等待 30 秒，得到我想要的 98% 的东西。

......因为你刚刚讲了一个轶事，表明事实是“如果有人使用人工智能化身，他们可能在人工智能出现之前就是你的客户”。

> 在我看来，艺术家应该在“堆栈”中向上移动。如果他们是委托艺术家，他们应该让客户以“98％的努力”来找他们，或者只接受对人工智能来说意味着太多、太复杂或其他敏感的委托。

这没有道理。这不是“向上移动堆栈”，而是工作枯竭，只剩下一小部分最困难/敏感的事情。这可能意味着你会被赶出你的职业，因为你没有足够的钱养活自己。

> Something I read elsewhere was "if someone is using an AI avatar, they were never going to be your customer anyway".

Is your point that what you quoted is false?

> I used to commission avatars every year or two from a specific artist. It wasn't super cheap (hundreds of dollars). At the end of the day though, spending hundreds of dollars, waiting weeks, and then maybe getting 85% of what I wanted doesn't make sense when I could instead spend ~$0, wait 30 seconds, and get 98% of what I want.

...because you just gave an anecdote that shows the truth is "if someone is using an AI avatar, they might have been your customer before AI".

> In my view, artists should be moving up the 'stack'. If they are a commission artist, they should be having customers come to them with their '98% efforts' or only taking on commissions that either mean too much, too elaborate for AI, or otherwise sensitive.

That doesn't make sense. That's not "moving up the stack," that's the work drying up and only a small remainder of the most difficult/sensitive things being left. And that might mean being driven out of your profession because there's not enough left for you to feed yourself.

在 HN 中查看
madamelic 2026/04/28 02:04
> 因为你刚刚讲了一个轶事，说明了事实：“如果有人使用人工智能化身，他们可能在人工智能出现之前就是你的客户”。

几年前我就不再委托艺术家制作头像，因为“它不是超级便宜（数百美元）。但最终，花费数百美元，等待数周，然后可能会得到我想要的 85%”

我厌倦了等待数周才得到诚实的中等结果。我不再购买艺术品，也不会再回去了，因为无论人工智能如何，经济学对我来说都没有意义。

> 只剩下一小部分最困难/敏感的事情

是的。随着技术的进步，这就是行业所发生的事情。马车司机和电梯操作员要么找到更专业的东西，要么离开这个行业。如果有人靠连体两件式的低额佣金谋生，并且不知道如何将其转化为行业中的其他东西，那么他们别无选择。

就我个人而言，我认为许多技术进步对人类来说是长期积极的，因为这意味着人类可以做一些比死记硬背更有意义的事情。这是反乌托邦和可怕的，但就我个人而言，这是艺术家转向更好的艺术的动力。

> because you just gave an anecdote that shows the truth is "if someone is using an AI avatar, they might have been your customer before AI".

I stopped commission artists for avatars years before that because of "It wasn't super cheap (hundreds of dollars). At the end of the day though, spending hundreds of dollars, waiting weeks, and then maybe getting 85% of what I wanted"

I got tired of waiting weeks only to get honestly a middling result. I stopped buying art and won't go back because the economics don't make sense to me regardless of AI.

> only a small remainder of the most difficult/sensitive things being left

Yep. It's what happens to industries as technologies progress. Horse carriage drivers and elevator operators either found something more specialized or moved out of the industry. If someone is making a living off onesie-twosie low-dollar commissions and can't figure out how to translate that to something else in the industry, they don't have any other choice.

Personally I think a lot of technology progression is long-term positive for humans because it means humans get to do something more fulfilling than rote work. It's dystopian and awful but personally, it's a shove for artists to move onto better art.

palmotea 2026/04/28 12:46
> 就我个人而言，我认为许多技术进步对人类来说是长期积极的，因为这意味着人类可以做一些比死记硬背工作更有意义的事情。这是反乌托邦和可怕的，但就我个人而言，这是艺术家转向更好的艺术的动力。

兄弟，你该更新你的表情包了。现在是 2026 年：技术进步意味着人类“开始”做更多死记硬背的工作，因为令人满意的创造性和智力方面都被自动化取代了。

让机器完成构建系统的创造性部分，这样您就不必这样做！相反，您会得到更多的代码审查！

> Personally I think a lot of technology progression is long-term positive for humans because it means humans get to do something more fulfilling than rote work. It's dystopian and awful but personally, it's a shove for artists to move onto better art.

Dude, you need to update your memes. It's 2026: technology progression means humans "get" to do more rote work as the fulfilling creative and intellectual aspects are automated away.

Have machines do the creative part of building the system, so you don't have to! Instead you get even larger piles of code reviews instead!

Quite a lot of people out there have made that decision. There's some sense of solidarity with artists, which makes sense, but I've seen plenty of angry messages about personal projects that were never going to pay an artist where they're still getting harangued with ridiculous sentiments like "just pick up a pencil yourself".

在 HN 中查看
wincy 2026/04/27 19:52
这些看起来不错。看来已经证实这些不是人工智能生成的。但我想说，即使他们是人工智能，我对他们是人工智能也没有任何问题。这是人们都很关心的楔子问题之一（当模型更多的是玩具并且有太多像稳定扩散这样的手指时，感觉对话更加微妙）但对我来说，这感觉就像有人在电动滑板车发明后没有用轿子载着某人穿过市场而生气。当然，踏板车的机动性不太好，你会失去一定的威严，但在大多数情况下它会完成大部分工作。

一种新工具的出现可以减少劳动力，并使以前遥不可及的东西变得人人都能获得。我并不真正关心失业的轿夫，我只关心实现我的目标。

市场肯定会在这里做出决定，就像 Photoshop 太好了一样不容错过，越来越多的你与之互动的艺术将由人工智能生成。聪明的艺术家只会对此撒谎，因为他们为什么不呢？

These look nice. It seems it’s been confirmed these aren’t AI generated. But want to say even if they were, I’d have no problem with them being AI at all. This is one of those wedge issues that people get all activated about (it felt like the conversation was more nuanced when the models were more of toys and had too many fingers like Stable Diffusion) but to me it feels analogous to someone being mad that someone isn’t being carried via palanquin through the market after the motorized scooter has been invented. Sure, the scooter isn’t quite as maneuverable and you lose a certain majesty, but it’ll get most of the job done in most of the cases.

A new tool exists that reduces labor and makes something previously out of reach accessible to everyone. I don’t really care about the unemployed palanquin operators, I just care about achieving my goals.

The market will definitely make the decision here and just like photoshop was just too good to pass up, more and more art you interact with is going to be AI generated. The smart artists will just lie about it, because why wouldn’t they?

在 HN 中查看
mschild 2026/04/27 21:21
我总体上不同意你的立场（尽管我尊重你的观点），并想就此向你提供不同的观点。可能需要一些时间来解释我的观点，所以请耐心等待。

将人工智能用于创意目的，特别是以创意输入为目标的创意目的，是我非常不喜欢的人工智能用法之一。艺术总是被人们看到并用来表达某些东西。它可能是情感，可能是观点，可能是政治观点，或者完全不同的东西。每个从事艺术的人都有其表演背后的意图。对于艺术家来说，意图可能并不总是显而易见的，有时意图是金钱，但它仍然存在。这种意图的最终结果也可能是好的艺术，也可能是坏的艺术。

对我来说，一件特定的艺术作品背后的想法是什么并不重要，只要有它就行。我可能不喜欢某件特定的艺术品，甚至不喜欢它背后的意图，但我也不必这样做。我什至可能不理解某个特定的部分，但这也没关系。

对于人工智能来说，没有意图。 AI 不会思考。它不知道为什么像素被放置在原来的位置，它只是偏离了它所训练的算法和数据。其背后没有想法、没有想法。

提示人工智能的人不是艺术家。他们不是创造者，无论他们在提示中付出多少努力，结果都不是他们的创造。人工智能不是传统意义上的工具（我们如何看待锤子或相机），它是一个执行者。如果我去 Fiverr 并告诉一个人为我创建一个图像，你会认为我是该图像的创建者吗？我不会，我想大多数其他人也不会。在平台上调试图像的过程甚至可能完全相同。您形成提示，向艺术家发送消息，获得结果，要求改进，直到您对结果满意为止。

I generally disagree with your stance (though I respect it as your opinion) and would like to offer you a different view on this. It might take a bit to explain my point so please bear with me.

Using AI for creative purposes, specifically ones where the creative input is the goal, is one usage of AI that I strongly dislike. Art has always been seen and used to express something. It could be emotions, it could be a perspective, it could be a political opinion, or something entirely different. Every person doing art has an intention behind their performance. The intent may not even always be obvious to the artist, and sometimes the intent is money, but its there nonetheless. The end result of that intent can also be good or bad art.

For me it doesn't really matter what the thought behind a specific piece of art is, as long as there was one. I may not like a specific piece of art or even the intention behind it, but I also don't have to. I may not even understand a specific piece but that's also fine.

With AI, there is no intent. The AI isn't thinking. It doesn't know why a pixel was placed where it was placed, its just going off an algorithm and data that it was trained on. There was no idea, no thought, behind it.

The person prompting the AI is not the artist. They are not the creator and no matter how much work they put into the prompt, the result is not their creation. AI is not a tool in the traditional sense of how we might view a hammer or a camera, its an executor. If I were to go to Fiverr and tell a person to create an image for me, would you consider me the creator of that image? I wouldn't and I think most other people wouldn't either. The process of commissioning an image on the platform might even be exactly the same. You form a prompt, send a message to an artist, get a result, ask for refinement until you're satisfied with the result.

mschild 2026/04/28 17:12
> 我不认为家长的评论认为他们是这样的，但是没关系。

我想他们做到了。他们特别说明了新工具如何可用，以及如何为以前无法​​使用的人们提供帮助。在创作艺术的背景下，这对我来说就像意味着提示者被认为是创造者。

> 你能认为电影导演是电影的创作者吗？他们只是告诉其他人该做什么。

如果从最字面意义上讲，导演所做的“唯一”事情就是指导，那么不，我不会。

虽然我不是媒体制作方面的专家，但从少量的采访中，我看到很多人也写剧本，并且参与的不仅仅是导演。

> I don't think that the parent comment argued that they are, but okay.

I think they did. They specifically said how a new tool is available and putting things into reach for people that previously wasn't. In the context about creating art, that reads to me like they do mean that prompters are considered the creators.

> Can you consider a movie director to be the creator of a movie? They are just telling other people what to do.

I'm not an expert in media production though but from the sparse amount of interviews I have seen a lot also write the scripts and are involved in a lot more than just directing.

I think ultimately this is just something we disagree on :)

在 HN 中查看
Peritract 2026/04/27 20:15
> 在大多数情况下它可以完成大部分工作

这对艺术来说并不是一个很高的标准。

尤其是在这种情况下，当前的艺术是对艺术的参考，并且对于艺术的粉丝来说，一切都与真实性有关。这也是一种产品上的艺术，它的目标并不是“直接完成”市场。

如果我只关心目的地，那么当然：使用最节省资源的方法。在这种情况下以及在所有其他需要考虑的情况下，将一切都简化为效率是荒谬的还原论。

> it’ll get most of the job done in most of the cases

This is not a very high standard for art.

Particularly not in this case, when the current art is a reference to, and for fans of, art that was all about authenticity. It's also art on a product that is very much not aiming for the 'just get it done' market.

If all I care about is the destination, then sure: use the most resource-efficient method. In this and in every other situation where there are other considerations, reducing everything to efficiency is absurdly reductionist.

在 HN 中查看
sokoloff 2026/04/27 20:19
有多少人家里有一幅《星夜》或《戴珍珠耳环的少女》的印刷品，而有多少人拥有手绘画布版（原件或复制品）？

在某种程度上，资源效率的显着提高可以改善许多事物的某些方面，甚至是艺术。

How many people have a print of “Starry Night” or “Girl with a Pearl Earring” in their house vs how many have a hand-painted on canvas edition (original or copy)?

At some point, a significant increase in resource efficiency improves certain aspects of many things, even art.

在 HN 中查看
wincy 2026/04/27 20:44
尽管《辛普森一家》被外包给韩国的动画师并使用数字工具来构图，但人们还是观看了《辛普森一家》。没有人抱怨马特·格勒宁没有手工制作每一帧的动画。

我使用 ChatGPT 根据我在脑海中思考了一段时间的故事概念为自己制作了一张照片。那张照片让我很高兴。二十年前它根本不存在。

我们现在看到的效率是从想法到执行的转变。我认为这是一件好事。我们现在要看到的是品味的策划。具有良好品味的人将成为在没有进入壁垒的市场中取得成功的人。我可以理解为什么这会让那些花了多年时间培养一项技能的人感到不安。

People watch The Simpsons despite it being farmed out to animators in Korea and using digital tools for the composition of the frames. Nobody is complaining that Matt Groening isn’t hand animating every frame.

I used ChatGPT to make myself a picture based on a concept of a story I’ve been kicking around in my head for awhile. That picture made me so happy. It just wouldn’t exist twenty years ago.

The efficiency we’re seeing now is in moving from idea to execution. I think that’s a good thing. The thing we’ll see now is curation of taste. People with good taste are going to be the ones to succeed in a market where there are no barriers to entry. I can understand why that would upset people who spent years cultivating a skill.

> At some point, a significant increase in resource efficiency improves certain aspects of many things, even art.

I'll agree with that incredibly-hedged claim, sure. I'm not against efficiency at all.

As before though, it's not the only consideration. It would have been even more efficient to give all the people with a copy of Girl with a Pearl Earring a blank canvas, or even nothing at all, but that would be missing the point.

Peritract 2026/04/27 22:30

我不是在谈论作品的出处，而是在谈论它的内容。

Again, I think you've misread the parent comment here. The Lord of the Rings--the actual books, the content of the work--is partially about authenticity, in the same way that Spiderman is about power and responsibility.

I'm not talking about the provenance of the work, but the content of it.

bcjdjsndon 2026/04/27 22:37

在 HN 中查看
Peritract 2026/04/27 23:15
老实说，我不确定我还能为你解释多少。我并不是想刁难你，但你却不断地误读和反对我没有说过的话。

指环王 涉及很多事情。其中一些是兽人。当我这么说的时候 指环王 是关于兽人的，我并不是说它是兽人制作的，也不是说兽人是用来分发它的，而是说兽人是文中讨论的东西。

同样，当我这么说时 指环王 部分是关于真实性，我不是在谈论它的写作方式，而是作品的内容。真实性是书中的一个主题，在文本中进行了讨论。

I'm honestly not sure how much more I can break this down for you. I'm not trying to be difficult here, but you keep on misreading and objecting to things I have not said.

The Lord of the Rings is about lots of things. Some of those things are orcs. When I say that The Lord of the Rings is about orcs, I'm not saying it's made by orcs, or that orcs were used to distribute it, but that orcs are something discussed within the text.

Similarly, when I say that The Lord of the Rings is partially about authenticity, I'm not talking about the way in which it was written, but the contents of the work. Authenticity is a theme in the books, discussed within the text.

lentil_soup 2026/04/27 21:10
> 市场一定会在这里做出决定

我不明白这一点，市场不是一个神圣的实体，你可以选择被动，让别人决定，但人们把“市场”推向他们认为正确的方向并没有错。大公司已经这样做了，所以人们完全可以引起人们的注意，反对它等等……这就是本文要做的事情

> The market will definitely make the decision here

I don't understand this, the market is not a divine entity, you can choose to be passive about it and let others decide, but there's nothing wrong with people pushing "the market" towards what they see is right. oneeyedpigeon 2026/04/27 20:24
> 这感觉就像有人在电动滑板车发明后，因为没有人用轿子载着穿过市场而生气

> it feels analogous to someone being mad that someone isn’t being carried via palanquin through the market after the motorized scooter has been invented

suddenlybananas 2026/04/27 20:06
您认为艺术是一种简单的商品吗？
Do you think art is a simple commodity?
在 HN 中查看
AlecSchueler 2026/04/27 20:11
大规模生产笔记本电脑的大型特许经营搭配绝对属于艺术领域的商品端。
A big franchise tie-in for mass produced notebooks is definitely on the commodity end of the artistic spectrum.
在 HN 中查看
wincy 2026/04/27 20:33
在这个具体的例子中，这种艺术模仿了《指环王》小说封面上的艺术作品。模仿本身就让它令人回味和怀旧。通常人们想要更多相同的东西。所以这正是属于商品的艺术。我认为这没有什么问题。

过去很多东西都是手工制作的。照顾和饲养马匹是一项受人尊敬的职业，每匹马都有不同的个性，但我们现在用汽车代替了。这并不意味着没有人养马，如果说有什么不同的话，这个职业已经变得更有声望，而不是一种商品，因为唯一养马的人是真正想养马的人。无论如何，我会骑自行车（如果可以的话），或者在买杂货时开车去商店。每次我使用货运自行车去买杂货时，我都不会想到养马人。

同样，我们都可以自由地出去花 8,000 美元购买由一棵老树雕刻而成的手工树脂河流桌面。它们很漂亮，我确实梦想过它们。 但一张非常漂亮的宜家木制厨房桌子，按照严格的规格制造，适合用途，价格仅为 899 美元。当我们将这些东西商品化时，我们失去的却是可及性和可负担性。这是一件好事，即使制造这些东西的人更少了。

最后一个例子，因为它是工业革命最大的催化剂之一，虽然我们仍然有人专门为金·卡戴珊 (Kim Kardashian) 制作高级定制服装，但这是一件好事，我们所有人都能获得 300 年前被认为难以置信的高质量的纺织品（从字面上看，织物的纱线密度和均匀性非常高）。

回想起来，我认为这些事情都很棒。

In this specific example, this art is mimicking the artwork on the fronts of the Lord of the Rings novels. The imitation itself is what makes it evocative and nostalgic. Often people want more of the same. So this is precisely the kind of art that is a commodity. I don’t think there’s anything wrong with that.

A lot of things used to be hand crafted. The care and raising of horses was a respected profession, each horse has a different personality, but we use cars instead now. That doesn’t mean nobody raises horses, if anything the profession has become more prestigious and less of a commodity because the only people raising horses are people who really want to raise horses. Regardless, I’m going to ride my bike (if I can), or drive my car to the store when I’m getting groceries. I’m not thinking about the horse breeders every time I use my cargo bike to get groceries.

Similarly, we’re all free to go out and spend $8,000 on artisanal resin river flow tabletop carved from a single old growth tree. They’re beautiful and I’ve certainly dreamed about it. But a very nice wooden IKEA kitchen table built to exacting specifications and fit for purpose is a mere $899. What we lose when commoditizing these things we gain in access and affordability. This is a good thing, even if there are fewer people making these things.

One last example, since it was one of the biggest catalysts of the Industrial Revolution, while we still have people making couture outfits for specifically for Kim Kardashian, but it’s a good thing that we all have access to textiles that would have been considered impossibly high quality (literally, the thread density and uniformity of the fabrics are so high) 300 years ago.

In retrospect these things are all pretty great, in my opinion.

ModernMech 2026/04/27 20:10

I think that "art" and "graphics on a book meant to sell merchandise to a fanbase" are different things and we have to start making that distinction more clear these days.

libertine 2026/04/27 20:25
因为行为所代表的一切，以及撒谎和欺骗作为一种美德的常态化。

我认为对于具有设计的产品来说，具有与人类相关的部分价值非常重要，但现实却截然不同：绝大多数人并不关心。

只要在亚马逊上看看那里的污水量，人们就会购买它 - 就好像我们现在的品味标准是如此之低，这有点悲伤，因为它只会变得更糟。

Because of everything that behavior represents, and the normalization of lying and deceit as a virtue.

I think it's important for a product with a design to have part of the value linked to a human, but the reality is quite different: the vast majority doesn't care.

Just go on Amazon a watch the volume of slop there, and people buy it - it's like our standards for taste are so low at the moment, it's a bit sad because it will only get worse.

在 HN 中查看
ottah 2026/04/27 22:44
> 因为行为所代表的一切，以及撒谎和欺骗作为美德的常态化。

你不觉得这有点双曲线吗？我们不是在谈论人们直接说谎。我们谈论的是人们以与十年前不同的方式制作图形媒体，并且不符合您的感受。

强制公开从来都不是艺术表达的要求。我知道有些人非常关心某物是如何制作的，但这并不意味着任何人都有义务披露用于制作作品的工具、媒体或来源。

我们唯一应该做的就是对选择披露的内容做出真实的陈述。如果一位艺术家告诉你，他们花了几个小时手工绘制一件作品，但事实并非如此，那么你就有可抱怨的地方了。不然不关你的事。

> Because of everything that behavior represents, and the normalization of lying and deceit as a virtue.

That's kind of hyperbolic don't you think? We're not talking about people stating a direct falsehood. We're talking about people producing graphic media in a different manner than ten years ago and that not aligning with your sensabilities.

A mandatory disclosure has never been a requirement of artistic expression. I know some people care a great deal about exactly how something is produced, but that doesn't mean anyone has ever had an obligation to disclose the tools, media or sources used to produce a work.

The only thing that we are due is truthful statements about what is chosen to be disclosed. If an artist tells you they spent hours hand painting a work and it was not, than you've got something to complain about. Otherwise it's none of your business.

wincy 2026/04/27 20:52
我的意思是，无论喜欢还是讨厌，大众从来不以其高雅品味而闻名。我想起那些卡尔文在保险杠贴纸和卡车坚果上撒尿的故事。我几乎不得不恳求我的妻子不要买一个标语，上面写着“我们不去裸泳，我们去厚重扣篮！”。

在 HN 中查看
futune 2026/04/27 20:56
我一直认为生成模型的任何输出都是不受版权保护的。

因此，如果一家公司生产的笔记本的唯一显着特征是用生成的图像进行装饰，那么其他任何人都将拥有合法权利大规模复制该笔记本并将完全相同的图像放在自己的笔记本上。

因此，如果一家公司想要制造实际的知识产权，那么他们需要雇用一个实际的人来生产它。

我很想听听是否有人知道： a) 此解释在任何相关司法管辖区是否准确？ b) 是否曾在法庭上进行过测试？

I always assumed that any output from a generative model would be uncopyrightable.

And hence, if a company produced, say, notebooks whose only distinguishing feature was being decorated with a generated image, then anyone else would be within their legal rights to copy it wholesale and put the exact same image on their own notebooks.

Therefore, if a company wants to manufacture actual intellectual property, then they need to hire an actual human to produce it.

I'd love to hear if anyone knows: a) Is this interpretation accurate in any relevant jurisdictions? b) Has it ever been tested in court?

在 HN 中查看
lentil_soup 2026/04/27 21:16
我认为很多人都误解了这一点。

这并不是指环王系列的随机搭配。 Moleskine 长期以来一直是艺术家的主要笔记本之一，正如文章提到的，他们的一些笔记本的封面上有艺术家的作品，他们的宣言都是关于“永恒的手写力量”和“用笔写在纸上，释放你独特的声音”等。拥有一家拥有如此声誉和与艺术界的联系的公司，与一些人工智能的合作来进行这次合作，对于自己的用户来说是一记耳光，因为他们可能会向毫无戒心的人出售一些笔记本。指环迷们。

这是一个曾经深受艺术家喜爱的品牌的“enshitification”。

I think a lot of people are misunderstanding this.

This is not some random tie-in to the LOTR franchise. Moleskine used to be one of the main notebooks for artists for a long time, and as the article mentions some of their notebooks have featured artists in their cover, their manifesto is all about "timeless power of handwriting" and "put pen to paper, and unleash your unique voice", etc. Having a company with that reputation and those connections to the art world just go with some AI slop for this collaboration is a slap to the face to its own users for potentially selling some notebooks to unsuspecting LOTR fans.

It's the enshittification of a brand once loved by artists.
